description:
GOAL: Get the bones of the CPS Cranelift backend up. Trampoline driver fn that loops on continuation pointers; one Cranelift fn per fz-IR fn taking a frame_ptr arg, returning a next_frame_ptr. Scalar ops (BinOp on ints, builtin calls) work; NO frame allocation yet (initial test fns have no live-across-call locals).

## Decisions captured

- Each fz-IR fn -> one Cranelift fn signature: `fn(*mut HeapHeader frame_ptr) -> *mut HeapHeader next_frame_ptr`.
- Trampoline: `while frame != null { frame = (jit_fn_for_frame.kind)(frame); }`. Runs in a Rust outer loop OR a Cranelift fn — choice deferred to implementation.
- Frame's HeapHeader.schema_id resolves to a Cranelift fn pointer via a fn-pointer table indexed by schema_id (or by a separate fn_id lookup if schemas are shared).
- For scalars (Int, Float, Bool, Atom): live in registers in the Cranelift fn body; only spilled to frame when liveness says so (.11.6 produces those decisions).
- This is NEW codegen (src/ir_codegen.rs). The OLD direct-style codegen (src/codegen.rs) is RIPPED OUT in .11.9 — green-field, no parallel paths, no feature flag (user decision).

## Deliverables (new src/ir_codegen.rs)

- pub fn lower_ir_module(module: &ir::Module) -> Result<CompiledModule, LowerError>
- Per-IR-fn Cranelift lowering: emit fn taking frame_ptr, decode args from frame, emit body for scalar ops + builtins.
- Trampoline emission (preferred: Cranelift fn for inlining; can start with Rust loop and migrate).
- Fn-pointer table: schema_id (or fn_id) -> compiled fn ptr.

## Tests

- `fn main() do print(40 + 2) end` runs through trampoline, prints 42.
- `fn main() do print(:ok) end` (atom literal).
- BinOp on ints; returns and halts.
- Builtin call (print) routes through correctly.

## Open / research

- Cranelift `call_indirect` performance hot in trampoline. Modern CPUs predict OK; measure with deep recursion in .11.8.
- Should the trampoline itself be a Cranelift fn (potentially inlinable into hot paths via .11.15 inliner) or a Rust loop? Start as Rust; promote to Cranelift if measurements warrant.

## Out of scope

- Frame allocation (.11.8).
- Heap types (.11.10+).
- Recursion that needs frames.
title: Cranelift codegen — trampoline + scalar ops
